blocks.body: A way to write very large or very small numbers as a product of a number between 1 and 10, and a power of 10. Form: a x 10^n, where 1 <= a < 10. Example: 3,400,000 = 3.4 x 10^6.

To convert to scientific notation: move the decimal so one non-zero digit is to the left. Count moves = exponent. Move left = positive exponent (large number). Move right = negative exponent (small number). Example: 0.00045 = 4.5 x 10^(-4).
To convert from scientific notation to standard form: move the decimal in the direction indicated by the sign of the exponent. Positive exponent = move right. Example: 6.2 x 10^4 = 62,000.
When multiplying in scientific notation: multiply the coefficients and add the exponents. (3 x 10^4)(2 x 10^5) = 6 x 10^9. If the result is not in proper form, adjust.
